title: rbac — trait composition
description: Four owned traits. `HasRoles` + `HasPermissions` are the composable traits every principal-type model uses to participate in RBAC — they wrap spatie's shipped traits and route the hot-path check through PermissionResolver. `HasResolvedPermissions` exposes a computed accessor for the cached compact permission array. `IsPermissionable` is a marker on models that policies protect — signals reflection-based tooling that the model is a permission target.

owned.description: Composable trait for principal-type models. EXTENDS `Spatie\Permission\Traits\HasRoles` and overrides `hasPermissionTo(...)` to route through `PermissionResolver::resolve()` — so RBAC ∪ grants − denies + delegation compose at query-time. Composed by User, PlatformUser, ServiceAccount.
owned.wraps: Extends spatie's HasRoles. Every method spatie ships continues to work; the ONE override is hasPermissionTo() which now goes through PermissionResolver. Compatible with `->assignRole()`, `->syncRoles()`, `->hasRole()`, `->hasAllRoles()`.

owned.description: Companion trait for direct permission grants (via model_has_permissions). Rare — usually roles carry perms. Composed alongside HasRoles on the same principal models.
owned.wraps: Same wrap pattern as HasRoles — extends spatie's HasPermissions with the resolver override.

owned.description: Exposes a computed accessor `->resolvedPermissions` returning the caller's full compact permission array (RBAC ∪ grants − denies). Cached per-request. Optional to compose — only for models that need programmatic access to the resolved set (e.g. custom controllers rendering permission-aware UI).
usedBy: identity::User (composed by default so /api/v1/auth/me can return the array)

owned.description: Marker trait signalling that a model is a permission TARGET (i.e. policies gate reads/writes on it, and access/grants can attach dynamic permissions to it). Adds no accessor / mutator / scope — pure metadata for reflection-based tooling (the access-request module's discovery scan uses it). Composed by every domain model with a policy.
usedBy: any model whose policy extends the standard shape

framework.reason: Role + Permission + RoleDefinition support soft-delete for the 90-day recovery window. Pivots are HARD-deleted (spatie's shipped behaviour).

notes: NO BelongsToTenant — tenant_id is nullable (null = system role); the resolver handles the nullable case explicitly.
HasActivityLog because role changes are audit-critical.
Same composition as Role. tenant_id nullable for the same reason.
Permissions are rarely deleted — soft-delete + hold indefinitely, per retention.json.
Platform-scoped metadata layer. Never deleted; only is_active=false.

pivots_common_note: The pivots (role_has_permissions, model_has_roles, model_has_permissions) do NOT compose traits — spatie's shipped pivot models are used as-is. Our observers attach for the enforcement logic. Denormalised (application_id, tenant_id) columns are populated by the observers on write.
